producedBy: skillfy (manual synthesis over knowledge/vault/snowflake-summit-2026/, deepen-skill contract)

skills.name: Snowpark for Python
skills.oneLine: Push Python compute to the data: lazy DataFrames that compile to SQL, UDFs/UDTFs, stored procedures.
notGoodAt: GPU-heavy deep-learning training loops (use external compute + Feature Store handoff)
useWhen: replacing pandas-on-laptop extracts that leak governance

skills.name: Snowflake Notebooks
skills.oneLine: Governed SQL+Python notebooks on live data — Git-backed, schedulable, no local extract.
useWhen: handing analysis to engineering without a rewrite

skills.name: Snowflake Cortex AI
skills.oneLine: LLM functions, Cortex Search (hybrid retrieval), Cortex Analyst (text-to-SQL) inside the governance boundary.
notGoodAt: bleeding-edge model access on day one
fine-grained inference cost control vs self-hosted vLLM
useWhen: text-to-SQL for business users with a semantic model

skills.name: Zero-Copy Cloning
skills.oneLine: Instant metadata-only clones — full-scale experiment sandboxes with no storage copy.
notGoodAt: isolating compute contention (clones share nothing but still cost compute to query)
useWhen: testing a feature pipeline against prod-scale data safely

skills.name: Snowflake Feature Store
skills.oneLine: Define features once, backfill, serve consistently to training and inference — kills train/serve skew.
notGoodAt: sub-10ms online feature serving at the edge
teams with no feature-reuse discipline (it's a contract, not magic)
useWhen: lineage from raw table to model input must be auditable

skills.name: Data Governance & Security (Horizon)
skills.oneLine: RBAC, dynamic masking, row-access policies, lineage, audit — governance as a platform property.
notGoodAt: substituting for a firm's own data-classification policy (it enforces one, doesn't write it)
useWhen: per-agent least-privilege design with AI Agent Identity

skills.name: Data Sharing & Marketplace
skills.oneLine: Live governed sharing without copies; third-party data (market, alternative) as native tables.
notGoodAt: sharing with counterparties outside Snowflake without reader accounts
useWhen: enriching models with vendor data without ETL

skills.name: The 2026 agentic layer (CoWork, Cortex Sense, Agent Identity)
skills.oneLine: Agents with cryptographic identity + per-agent RBAC + audit, grounded by an enterprise-memory context layer (83% vs 24% accuracy).
notGoodAt: anything ungoverned — the whole point is that agent quality is a context+governance property, not a model property
GA-maturity assumptions (Horizon Context and parts of the stack are still preview)
useWhen: designing enterprise agents that must pass a security review
explaining why context engineering beats model swapping
